paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Nadab and Abihu, the sons of Aaron, took each of them his censer, and put fire therein, and laid incense thereon, and offered strange fire before Jehovah, which he had not commanded them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And there came forth fire from before Jehovah, and devoured them, and they died before Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Then Moses said unto Aaron, This is it that Jehovah spake, saying, I will be sanctified in them that come nigh me, and before all the people I will be glorified. And Aaron held his peace.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan, the sons of Uzziel the uncle of Aaron, and said unto them, Draw near, carry your brethren from before the sanctuary out of the camp.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: So they drew near, and carried them in their coats out of the camp, as Moses had said.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses said unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons, Let not the hair of your heads go loose, neither rend your clothes; that ye die not, and that he be not wroth with all the congregation: but let your brethren, the whole house of Israel, bewail the burning which Jehovah hath kindled.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And ye shall not go out from the door of the tent of meeting, lest ye die; for the anointing oil of Jehovah is upon you. And they did according to the word of Moses.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Aaron, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Drink no wine nor strong drink, thou, nor thy sons with thee, when ye go into the tent of meeting, that ye die not: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and that ye may make a distinction between the holy and the common, and between the unclean and the clean;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and that ye may teach the children of Israel all the statutes which Jehovah hath spoken unto them by Moses.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses spake unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons that were left, Take the meal-offering that remaineth of the offerings of Jehovah made by fire, and eat it without leaven beside the altar; for it is most holy;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and ye shall eat it in a holy place, because it is thy portion, and thy sons` portion, of the offerings of Jehovah made by fire: for so I am commanded.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the wave-breast and the heave-thigh shall ye eat in a clean place, thou, and thy sons, and thy daughters with thee: for they are given as thy portion, and thy sons` portion, out of the sacrifices of the peace-offerings of the children of Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The heave-thigh and the wave-breast shall they bring with the offerings made by fire of the fat, to wave it for a wave-offering before Jehovah: and it shall be thine, and thy sons` with thee, as a portion for ever; as Jehovah hath commanded.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses diligently sought the goat of the sin-offering, and, behold, it was burnt: and he was angry with Eleazar and with Ithamar, the sons of Aaron that were left, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Wherefore have ye not eaten the sin-offering in the place of the sanctuary, seeing it is most holy, and he hath given it you to bear the iniquity of the congregation, to make atonement for them before Jehovah?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Behold, the blood of it was not brought into the sanctuary within: ye should certainly have eaten it in the sanctuary, as I commanded.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Aaron spake unto Moses, Behold, this day have they offered their sin-offering and their burnt-offering before Jehovah; and there have befallen me such things as these: and if I had eaten the sin-offering to-day, would it have been well-pleasing in the sight of Jehovah?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And when Moses heard [that], it was well-pleasing in his sight.
